title: 20+ years as an Amazon customer
body: 20+ years as an Amazon customer. Thousands spent. Never an issue… until now.In the last year:	•	A robot lawnmower arrived with a bag of sand in the box	•	A faulty vacuum returned under manufacturer advice	•	A £5 item marked as delivered that never arrivedAll genuine issues. All outside my control.Now I’m being warned my account isn’t compliant with their refund policy — with no real explanation, just automated responses.So let me get this straight…Items don’t arrive or arrive tampered with, I report it, and somehow I’m the problem?Loyalty clearly counts for very little.Disappointing doesn’t quite cover it.

title: My account was hacked!
body: My account was hacked and the e-mail and phone number were changed.  I have had this account for 20 years.  I called Amazon and they said they would get back with me in 24-48 hours.  That was over a week ago.  Tons of reviews like this on FB with no one helping them. I can go through my email and pull every order from my account, but they cannot find my account at all now?  I have paid for prime, countless movies, audibles, and have a bunch of CC on my account.  My son cannot access any of his games on his Kindle.  They need to fix this and help people ASAP. For a multi-BILLION dollar company to do nothing or help people with something like this is insane! I will be looking into to other options after this awful and unprofessional experience.

title: Do NOT give Amazon your money
body: My boyfriend had a subscribe and save subscription for Monster. When we were having issues with money, we canceled it. Each month, we kept receiving the canceled Monster, so we would check, and sure enough what we canceled is back on his account. We went as far as canceling it with a live agent over the phone, and now he has another four cases. They're very poor excuses for scammers. It's obvious they're doing it to scam money out of you. Why else would an app have such poor quality control, unhelpful agents and care for their customers. Now, we're trying to return the Monster, like we've done ever time, because we can't be spending 40+ dollars on that stuff, we need it for rent. And they're denying us of the return. Every single time we got these drinks when we weren't supposed to, we got a refund. Now they're trying to pull some backwards shit on us saying that they have to be damaged or something in order for our money to be returned.  These people are complete imbeciles for thinking they could slip this past me and I would turn a blind eye, I am going to do everything in my power to go against Amazon and everything they own.

title: For the sake of your sanity.
body: I made the mistake of ordering a tumble dryer from Amazon. As I'm getting on in years I also ordered their "Unpack and Haul Away" service whereby Amazon deliver the tumble dryer to the appropriate room, unpack it a'd haul away the packaging And the old tumble dryer for recycling.I place my orders on 26/3/26 and it was delivered two days later. Unfortunately the delivery team said they knew nothing about hauling anything anywhere and promptly left.Since then I have telephoned Amazon almost every day, each and every time the agent promises the haulage team will contact me in 24 hours and each time nobody calls or emails or even texts me. Each time I call it's met with profusely apologies and bewilderment as to how they keep failing to honour their contract. The last time was yesterday when I spoke to a young lady who promised this time would be different as she would personally monitor the situation until I received my call. Still nothing.I have lost all faith in Amazon, I could have ordered the same items with three other companies but opted for Amazon as I trusted them and have been a loyal customer for many years.I will never make that mistake again.I hope Amazon read this, because their internal communications are clearly pitiful. It's not a case of the left hand not knowing what the right hand is doing, the left hand doesn't even know it has a right hand.

title: Amazon Is Straight-Up Stealing From Customers
body: I am absolutely furious with Amazon’s service and how they treat their customers.I ordered a suit, and the order status showed “delivered” — except I NEVER received it. I also never shared the OTP with anyone, which is required for delivery. So clearly, something went wrong on their end.When I first contacted customer service, they assured me I would get a refund. But after waiting 2–3 days and receiving nothing, I reached out again — only to be told that after their “investigation,” the item was marked as successfully delivered.I explained repeatedly that I never gave the OTP and never received the package, but they simply didn’t care. Every single time I contacted them — even speaking to a supervisor — I got the same robotic response: “The product was delivered.” No accountability, no proper investigation, nothing.This is completely unacceptable. If a customer hasn’t shared an OTP, how can you claim the delivery was valid?Amazon has basically taken my money and refused to fix their mistake.DO NOT ORDER FROM AMAZON. They don’t protect their customers and clearly don’t care when things go wrong. This feels nothing short of being scammed.

title: Amazon Doesn’t Care About the Working…
body: Amazon Doesn’t Care About the Working Man — And the Clock Is Ticking on People Like JoshPosted April 6, 2026I’ll be honest upfront: I missed the return window. I know. I owned it. I apologized. But what followed revealed exactly who Amazon is when policy is on their side and you have nothing left to leverage.The item — a CyberPower UPS, still sealed, never opened, never touched — sat in original packaging because life buried me. My grandfather passed away. My mother was diagnosed with cancer. I was fighting depression, drowning in school deadlines, managing financial pressure, and my car was dead in the driveway with a failed ABS brake module I couldn’t afford to fix. I wasn’t being careless. I was surviving.I asked for anything reasonable — store credit, account credit, a goodwill exception, something. I wasn’t demanding. I was asking a billion-dollar company to act human for sixty seconds.Josh, the supervisor I was escalated to, said no. Every way I asked. No creativity. No empathy. No attempt to actually help. Just policy repeated back like a shield.Here’s what Josh and people like him don’t seem to realize yet: the same technology that’s reshaping every other industry is coming for customer service management too. The idea that a supervisor role — one that amounts to reading policy and saying no — is somehow untouchable? That era is ending. AI doesn’t get tired. It doesn’t clock out. And frankly, in this interaction, it would have shown more flexibility and human decency than Josh did.Amazon calls itself customer-obsessed. But customer obsession apparently expires with your return label. After that, you’re on your own — grieving, sick with worry about your mother, broke, and being told there’s nothing anyone can do by someone who made zero effort to find out if that’s actually true.This wasn’t about the money. It was about being treated like a person. Amazon failed. Completely.

title: In search of shoes, this soul wandered through the "Source Of Hope".
body: Steel Toe Cap Trainers Work Safety TrainersBrand :BAGHUYA soul once laboured beneath the weight of endless toil, the purse reduced to withered husk, graced only by meagre offerings drawn from the land. Sickness came as ravenous beast, consuming what little coin could be scraped together, leaving the spirit battered and the pockets bare. the lovely shades of periwinkle and rose whispered of expenses scarcely afforded, pressing down upon what little remained.In search of shoes, this soul wandered through the "Source Of Hope". The green boots came first, yet my boy stumbled and fell in them far too often. Then came the temptation of fashionable white ones, so stylish and so desired, but the very stones seemed to cling to them, a constant annoyance with every hurried stride.Now, these boots: steel-toed and broad, a promised sanctuary. Yet, even as held, a protest arose. His toes were cramped, confined, pinched. Hark, crimson doth approach! They vowed protection, a shield from harm. But could this pledged safety keep him whole?The makers spoke of guarding against the sharp teeth of hard labour, of warding off every painful prick. Their word was taken, trusting they would spare Nick this trial. Light as a whisper, they claimed, feather-light and weightless. In this, at least, a truth. He found them light, delightfully light.Their soles gripped treacherous paths, clinging to anything that might send him sprawling. Young Nick had held his footing thus far.Yet still, a bitter truth gnawed away: that choosing the cheaper path, driven by desperation, often leads to far greater ruin than a modest, initial investment. It was painfully clear, and in that clarity burned a fire within those who simply tried to survive, to make their way, one struggling page at a time.

title: This complaint is about Amazon Prime service
body: This complaint is about Amazon Prime orders and how they resolve shipping issues. The references to USPS are part of the problem - however Amazon's handling is why this review is here. For reference - this has occurred on more than one occasion. I have an Amazon Prime account which I pay a yearly fee to be a part of.  Smart shoppers can place an order and take advantage of free shipping (and in some cases 1-2 day free shipping. To insure you benefit from this, its important to make sure the listing states "Prime" shipping in addition to placing your order in a timely manner to insure this happens.I placed an order that promised 2 day shipping (arriving today). When I placed the order, I confirmed that the seller offered prime shipping and item was in stock. Selecting the prime shipping option -  I am not given a choice of which shipper to use, and since the shipping is "free", I have  no remedy other than calling Amazon and hoping for the best.   Sadly - In several other cases, Amazon has credited my account only to charge me months later for the item I may have had a shipping issue with never gets returned or the order isn't properly processed (i.e. Amazon says I don't need to return the item).--THAT SAID - Onward to my recent order and most recent subject of this review . My order was listed via USPS as out for delivery.  Later on the same day - USPS claims "Customer has requested the shipment to be held for collection. Please collect your package once available for pick-up." - to be clear - I DID NOT request any such hold from USPS.  With USPS closed there is NO way to reach them, AND my only remedy was to contact Amazon to resolve this. An let us not forget the 2 day prime delivery is now NOT going to occur. -- Calling Amazon and escalating required 4 calls with requests for a supervisor. On each call I needed to request a supervisor 2-3 times before reaching  a Supervisor. 3 supervisors later  I was given a full refund of my product and advised Amazon will handle returning the item without my intervention. For the record - The first 2 supervisors claimed I could not get a return processed until 4 additional days passed (hmm... 2 day delivery failure yet no resolution for twice that amount of time) NO CUSTOMER should EVER have to do this. Shame on you Amazon.   Finally - although my issue has been "resolved" - I now have to watch my credit card statements with a magnifying glass to be sure I don't get charged for this in the future. - Uggh.

title: Guarantee Delivery
body: I’ve had a Prime membership for several years and order from Amazon often. Most of the time I just let items arrive whenever. But recently I needed certain parts quickly, so I used their “guaranteed next‑day delivery” options — the ones that promise 4–8 AM or 11–3 PM if you order before the cutoff time.I met the cutoff, the order was confirmed, and the guarantee was shown on my screen. Long story short: it’s now the next day, it’s 1:46 PM, and I still have no package. That guarantee clearly means nothing.If I had a shop appointment to get my vehicle repaired, I would have been completely out of luck. No parts, missed appointment, and now I’d have to wait another week or more to get another slot.A company this big shouldn’t offer guaranteed delivery windows if they can’t honor them. It creates a bad experience and damages trust. Good thing I do my own repairs in the automotive field, or this would have cost me even more time and money.

title: Amazon South Africa has my Macbook and money
body: I purchased a MacBook (Order #408‑8682143‑0966718) through Amazon South Africa which had a latent component failure. In February 2026 it began freezing.I shared Apple’s report with Amazon on 16 February, and on 5 March 2026, their supervisor confirmed I qualified for a full refund. The laptop was returned and acknowledged on 13 March, yet as of 1 April 2026, I still have not received repayment of R20 899.Over several weeks, I’ve spoken with multiple agents — and have two active case numbers (D409704577 plus another opened 31 March). I’ve spent hours on hold, repeatedly told the issue would be “escalated,” yet nothing moves forward. One even admited that as of 14 March the escalation ticket has not moved one bit forward.As a postgraduate student relying on this laptop for academic work, I find it deeply disappointing that a company promoting itself as “Earth’s Most Customer‑Centric” cannot resolve a verified faulty‑product refund after multiple written confirmations.I hope Amazon takes this as constructive feedback, resolves my refund promptly, and improves its accountability to customers in South Africa

title: It's time to stop using Amazon
body: I would give a negative rating if at all possible. There is no accountability for their customer service reps. There is no ability to escalate a problem when the overseas call center cannot provide a solution. On March 10th, my business account was locked, stating that my credit card company was disputing charges dating back to November of 2025, when neither my credit card company nor I had disputed any charges from Amazon. On this one day, Amazon incorrectly stated that I was disputing close to 100 charges when these items were paid for and well received by me.I now have over 20 hours of writing emails and speaking with ineffective call center Representatives trying to regain access to my account. I now have over 1,000 AI generated emails from Amazon. They have several different variations but the one I love best beginsThe card ending in 00 that you approved us to charge has not been added to your Amazon account.I'm dying to know how they were able to charge my card if it has not been added to my Amazon account.I have responded thoughtfully by email over a dozen times, hoping that at some point someone might read my communication, but in each instance, I receive either an AI-generated, incorrect response or a human response from someone whose IQ is in the single digits.The recurring proposed solution is that I update my credit cards in my account, even though I've been blocked and cannot access it.In addition to the significant amount of wasted time on my part, this has resulted in a major disruption to my business's workflow, as we have been placing orders with Amazon at least once a week, if not several times, and have had over 40 Subscribe & Save orders. We have now had to find new vendors for all of these products.I think it's time for everyone to consider dropping companies like Amazon that demonstrate no business integrity or respect for their customers.And let's face it, from a social standpoint, they're a horrible company who act very much from a bully mentality owned by a world-class narcissistic a..holeThere are many alternatives to Amazon that place a strong value on customer service and the relationship they have with their customers.Over the years, I have purchased over 1,500 ebooks from Amazon, and I'm now switching to Bookshop.org as I explore the many alternatives to Amazon, which has made great efforts to provide great customer service and act with integrity.Amazon has a Trustpilot rating of 1.7, and no company should be in business whose customers hold them in such horrible regard.I hope the countless individuals who have been consistently and repeatedly strongly dissatisfied with their Amazon experience will stop giving any business to Amazon in the future.

title: Customer Concerns Don’t Matter to Amazon
body: “I am a longtime Amazon customer who spends a substantial amount of money each year, and I am extremely frustrated with repeated issues in their delivery, return, and customer service processes. Packages frequently arrive days late, and in some cases are reported as delivered — including notifications from Alexa — before they actually arrive. Returned items that I have followed all instructions to send back are consistently marked as not received. Weeks later, I receive notifications that charges will be reapplied to my account, despite providing proof of the return.Customer service representatives routinely fail to listen to what I am explaining. They often repeat questions I’ve already answered or ask irrelevant questions, and it seems they are following a script rather than actually listening. It is painfully obvious that the training Amazon provides their representatives falls far short — their employees are neither empowered nor prepared to resolve recurring issues.Amazon’s quality control and quality assurance processes are clearly failing, as I continue to experience late deliveries, mishandled returns, and unhelpful customer service. These systemic failures create unnecessary stress and inconvenience for customers who follow all proper procedures, and give the strong impression that, although Amazon is both big and convenient, it seems they don’t feel the need to worry about losing customers.”

title: Formal Complaint, Request for Investigation, Compensation
body: Dear Amazon Customer Service,I am writing to raise a formal complaint regarding two serious delivery failures that occurred with my recent orders, as well as to request compensation for the inconvenience, delay, and stress caused.First, my parcel scheduled for delivery today was delivered to the wrong address and handed to an unknown individual. I have no information about who received my item, and I was not contacted at any point. This is completely unacceptable and raises concerns about the security and accuracy of your delivery process.Second, the delivery for my order yesterday was left outside my front door without any attempt to knock or notify me. I only became aware of the delivery because I received a notification stating that the item had been delivered. Leaving parcels unattended in this way poses a significant risk, as anyone could have taken the item.Although I have been informed that a redelivery will take place within the next 24 hours, this does not resolve the disruption caused. I was expecting my items today, and now I am forced to wait due to errors that were entirely avoidable. This is the second delivery failure in 24 hours, which is extremely concerning.I would like to highlight the following Amazon delivery policies, which have not been followed:- Deliveries must be made to the correct address. If a parcel is delivered to the wrong address, Amazon acknowledges this as a service error.- Parcels should not be handed to unknown individuals. Handing a parcel to someone who is not the customer, a household member, or an appropriate neighbour is not permitted.- Drivers are expected to attempt contact by knocking or ringing the doorbell. This did not happen.- Parcels should only be left in a safe place, and only when the customer has designated or approved it. My front door is not a secure location, and I did not authorise this.- Amazon remains responsible for the parcel until it is delivered correctly to the intended recipient.Given the repeated failures and the inconvenience caused, I am requesting appropriate compensation. While a redelivery has been arranged, it does not address the delay, the stress caused, or the fact that your delivery standards were not met on two consecutive occasions.I also request that this matter be investigated urgently and that steps are taken to prevent this from happening again.For escalation purposes, I am aware that Amazon’s Executive Customer Relations team can be contacted, and I will consider escalating this matter if necessary.I look forward to your prompt response and a satisfactory resolution.

One time a bot sent a link for me to verify my account, but it did not work! All I wanted was to understand why they cancelled the orders rather than delivering them a day later. I would advise not to put in multiple orders on the same day.  And even with a Prime membership, not to rely on delivery targets.

title: Don’t Trust AZ Guarantee on Amazon
body: As a rule Amazon are good but when it comes the third party guarantee AZ don’t assume you are safe because you are not as I found out but luckily I have not lost out on this .I ordered a solar panel kit from a company and a few days later recieved just a single item from the order and it stated on the site item delivered when in a way it was but only one item was sent so I sent a message through Amazon stating what had happened and given this is almost £400 I was a little concerned but not overly worried as I thought Amazon had my back so to speak .The following day Exo Worthy messaged to say don’t be worried the rest of the shipment will arrive this is normal .I waited and sure enough another single item arrived and then thanks Evri the last of the items arrived .Now I was relived that the delay which had caused most of this issue came from Evri and a lame excuse they couldn’t get access to property ( rubbish and heard before ) Now all the goods arrived thanks in part to Trust Pilot The matter is closed and I advised Amazon that I wasAt last in receipt of the goods paid for .Today out of the blue I have been advised my claim is denied and I would not have been covered by the guarantee I’m not the first that this has happed to and my ire isn’t pointed at anyone accept Amazon for allowing this practice and Evri for the usual fire service I have since looked on eBay and checked the feedback of this company which to be fair have been ok in this case but when thier customers had the same experience thier money wasRefunded as it should have been although in my case I just wanted my goods . Poor choice of couriers and very poor dispatch system although made up in partBy customer service but if it had all gone wrong I would not have got my £400 back . This is just a warning to anyone caring to read it don’t rely on Amazon and the AZ guarantee over this as it shone a light on what to expectWhen things go wrong I’m seriously toying with dumping them

title: Amazon allows repeated fraud by third-party sellers and fails to protect buyers
body: I purchased a Lenovo Yoga Pro 9i laptop on Amazon Marketplace for $2,427.70. The product page displayed "Visit the Lenovo Store" — I had no idea I was purchasing from a third party. The laptop arrived from a seller called Montana Joe operated by Techdeals in Brooklyn NY. Before setting it up I checked the Lenovo warranty using the serial number. The warranty had been activated 31 days before my purchase date proving it was pre-owned and fraudulently listed as new. Amazon customer service confirmed there was no fraud department to escalate to and that returning to the fraudulent seller was my only option. Multiple other buyers have reported identical fraud from this same seller. I had to file an FTC report and an IC3 complaint to get any traction. Amazon needs to improve third-party seller disclosure and fraud handling policies to protect buyers.

title: I want to applaud Amazon for their…
body: I want to applaud Amazon for their excellent customer service!. I ordered an item and after the purchase, the delivery date showed later than initially advertised. After contacting their CS team, they sourced the item, prioritised it, and got it delivered on time. I'm really happy with how quickly they dealt with the issue!

title: Amazon Blink camera system
body: Amazon Blink camera system, outside in particular, when it works, it’s not too bad. The system tends to revert to “cloud” storage , which I detest! Do not want subscriptions, cloud storage or otherwise . I have been using various outside cameras for over a year. Unreliable. Inconsistent recordings/triggers. I realize certain locations are not ideal to place, but in our neighborhood, there are few locations that work well. It’s the triggering and recording that is my issue. I would recommend looking elsewhere. I have contacted tech support more than once without any real resolution. The system seemed to “correct “ itself. Too much time spent troubleshooting. Also, the “requirement “ that you use lithium only batteries is unreasonable as several cameras run those batteries down quickly without being used. Do some homework before purchasing the Amazon Blink camera system. I didn’t. Paying for it now!

title: HOW TO MAKE A 5* REVIEW A 0* REVIEW
body: YET ANOTHER should be 0* review.I honestly believe Amazon know what is going on with its couriers/deliveries, especially when the courier is good enough to take a photo.Given a delivery window of between 7pm-9pm, I found the delivery notification online, when randomly checking (I wasn't buzzed/called)  I found a pile of packages (3) for 3 different people, dumped just inside the door of our block, IN THE FOYER...AGAIN.AGAIN, DUMPED...IN A PUBLIC PLACE, WITHOUT NOTIFICATION.A nice photo is available, BUT I see no way to upload it Trustpilot?SOI just went to leave feedback (on the Amazon site) for this delivery, only to find my (OLD/LAST 5* (for making it to my door with the last delivery) review was rejected, because? "Your review doesn't meet our community guidelines. Please edit and resubmit"THIS IS THAT REVIEW"A G8 (cheaper than most supermarkets OWN BRAND) price, and speedy Prime TO MY DOOR delivery is what helped make this purchase 5*.It's not often I get to say this, but delivery (and the driver) were A1. The driver actually took the time to deliver TO ME...on the 1st floor, and not (as is often the case) dump the delivery in the Foyer of the flats, without any notification, so, well done that man...well done Amazon.Heinz soups are still one of the best, and this is absolutely the best price/place I've ever found it, so I always take the opportunity to stock up, when I find these deals, and that always seems to be with Amazon"It's like I said..."how to make a 5* review a 0* review"

title: I am looking at cancelling my Amazon…

Multiple chats for a refund due (2, 12 packs of theatre size Nerds candy, only 1 arrived), all bullshitters saying same, i.e., "Don't worry, I am here to help." Filed BBB complaint as this is not 1st time with negative experience; IT WILL BE THE LAST. Must be training people to deny, deny and hope they give up ..not me

title: Big Shop of Horrors
body: A shop that stocks such an amaz-ing volume of absolute senseless trash, it might as well be called Big Shop of Horrors. If this "retailer" has a head buyer like most decent businesses do, well this one must be an absolute imbecile, and I mean that in a clinical sense.

title: Some Amazon Drivers
body: I am so sorry to give this score as this is not all drivers. The driver for my envelopes only delivered 1 and not the other. He marked that he delivered both. There were 2 packages for another lady and 1 envelope for me so I thought both bday cards were in the same envelope. So when he marked both items delivered he lied as there was only one my nephews card in the envelope. Disgusting service how would they not like to received their items. This is not a house this is an apartment building. It is unacceptable to leave them outside. If they had rung my apartment I would have let them in and they could have left them in the lobby. Anyone could have taken them. Please I am sure most of your customers feel the same way. Stop leaving them and not alerting customers. What happened if I was not home or out of town. Disgraceful service. Extra information in reference to your email on Sat March 28th 2025In reference to your email about my complaint I don’t live in a single family home I live in an apartment building with 52 apartments in it so the garbage about single family home and my front door is incorrect it was left at the front door of the building. Here is some information for you to think about and make up more untruths. If someone puts instructions they should be followed you are a delivery company so deliver the packages to the correct addresses and the correct door not the front door of an apartment building. Please read below. These are correct facts to the in correct information you wrote to me in an email. * Good morning First of all I didn’t ask your driver to leave anything at the front it clearly states that they should call my apartment. It is not a single family home it is an apartment building. Secondly it states that I can’t get around so would appreciate your drivers following simple instructions. Not that difficult.Thirdly they left 3 packages there were 2 packages for apartment number 35 and I was told that there should have been 2 envelopes for number 29. Your driver didn’t deliver the correct number of packages. In conclusion I have fractured the neck of my femur I had it repaired with a dynamic hip screw and I had a liver transplant last year in Oct which I can prove. If your drivers can’t follow simple instructions then there is no hope. You pay for delivery not to be left somewhere and schmuck could have taken the packages. Nottinghamshire police have asked the public that Amazon are doing this it needs to stop. I know mine were just bday cards but it was for my brother’s bday today as he was supposed to pick his card up on Friday 27th so  thank you for ruining his bday. You should give yourselves a round of applause 👏 as you have again fail to follow a verbal contract. So the information below single family home is a lie and it wasn’t left at my front door that picture is the entrance door to the building. But you have obviously lied.

title: A €1000 MacBook that says it requires…
body: A €1000 MacBook that says it requires signature on delivery was just dumped in the communal hallway of our building. Says handed to resident on the tracking which is clearly a lie and no signature from us so I would not trust them with any delivery at all.

title: Still the easiest place to buy stuff
body: Still the easiest place to buy stuff, but yeah… you really have to check seller ratings now. Not everything is reliable like before

title: Amazon - No customer care!
body: Amazon, the worst ever experience. I purchased an item. They stated that my payment card was not processing payment. I cancelled that card and registered another card from another bank and purchased the item.                The next day, I noticed two withdrawals from my banks.   I tried for over two weeks to cancel the orders with Amazon, logging well over two hours on chatting, being passed through around a minimum of ten people! I phoned twice, being passed from pillar to post and held for around an hour, with still, no result or satisfaction. I had to email their Executive customer care...to this day, they have returned one transaction, £298 but not the other one £312.44.I am still trying to get my refund!The customer care at Amazon is a joke.

body: Great customer servicer, excellent experiance, 5 stars
